title: Preschool Number Sense Activity
subject: Early Mathematics
prerequisites: Count aloud from one to five.
Point to one object at a time while counting.
summary: A gentle activity sheet for counting, matching, and comparing small quantities.
learning_objectives: Match small quantities to written numerals.
Compare groups using more and fewer language.
curriculum_references: Early years number sense: counting and cardinality

standards: Early years number sense: counting and cardinality
items.prompt: Draw a line from each group of dots to the matching number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5.
items.answer: Each group should be matched to the numeral showing the same number of dots.
items.explanation: This checks whether learners can connect counted objects to written numerals.
rubric: Credit matching each group to the correct numeral.

standards: Early years number sense: quantity comparison
items.prompt: Circle the group that has more objects: two stars or four stars.
rubric: Credit circling the group with four stars.

standards: Early years number sense: one-to-one correspondence
items.prompt: Ask the learner to count five classroom objects aloud.
items.answer: The learner should count one object per number word up to five.
items.explanation: Listen for skipped objects, repeated counts, or mismatched pointing.
rubric: Credit counting aloud in the correct order.
